cases.governingRule: Multiply every term inside the parentheses by the outside factor.

pedagogy.hint: Check whether the outside factor reached every term inside the parentheses.
pedagogy.explanation: The distributive property scales each term in the grouped expression before you simplify.

fallback.hint: Inspect how the factor outside the parentheses applies to each inside term.
fallback.explanation: Use the distributive property, then verify that the new equation has the same solution set.

cases.governingRule: Each transformation must preserve the same solution set.

pedagogy.hint: Compare what changed on both sides and ask whether the same values still satisfy the equation.
pedagogy.explanation: A valid transformation must keep the equation balanced and preserve exactly the same solution set.

fallback.hint: Check whether this move keeps both sides balanced.
fallback.explanation: Every transformation must preserve exactly the same solution set.

cases.governingRule: Apply the same inverse operation to both sides.

pedagogy.hint: Your transformations preserve the solution set.
pedagogy.explanation: Each inverse operation was applied consistently, leaving the variable isolated.

fallback.hint: Recheck each inverse operation on both sides.
fallback.explanation: Equivalent steps preserve the same solution set while isolating the variable.
